Name: Three-Valued Naive Set Theory
Description: Rulebook formalizing naive set theory with three-valued (Strong Kleene) membership and Rule 12 (NULL membership), which dissolves Russell's paradox into a single ungrounded membership fact.

TruthValues.Description: The three truth values a membership question or condition can take: True, False, or NULL. Admitting NULL makes membership a partial, non-bivalent semantics rather than the classical two-valued one; bivalence is recovered wherever a definition is grounded.

schema.name: TruthValueId
schema.Description: Stored identity of the truth value: 'true', 'false', or 'null'.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display alias for the truth value.

schema.name: Symbol
schema.Description: Single-letter symbol: T, F, or N.

schema.name: Glyph
schema.Description: Logical glyph for the value.

schema.name: Rank
schema.Description: Strong Kleene order used by min/max: True=1, NULL=0, False=-1.

schema.name: IsClassical
schema.Description: True for the two bivalent values (True/False); False for NULL.

schema.name: IsUndefined
schema.Description: True only for NULL, the undefined value meaning 'does not converge'.

schema.name: CountOfFacts
schema.Description: How many membership facts resolve to this value.

schema.name: FactsWithValue
schema.Description: Membership facts that resolve to this truth value.

Connectives.Description: Logical connectives whose Strong Kleene (K3) truth tables define how NULL propagates. This is the 'gap' route (NULL where evaluation fails to converge), the sibling of paraconsistent 'glut' logics, and it follows Kripke's fixed-point grounding.

schema.name: ConnectiveId
schema.Description: Stored identity: 'negation', 'conjunction', or 'disjunction'.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display alias.

schema.name: Symbol
schema.Description: Operator glyph (not / and / or).

schema.name: Arity
schema.Description: Number of inputs: 1 for negation, 2 for conjunction/disjunction.

schema.name: KleeneRule
schema.Description: How the K3 output is computed (negate / min / max over ranks).

schema.name: CountOfTruthTableRows
schema.Description: Rows in this connective's truth table.

schema.name: TruthTableRows
schema.Description: The truth-table rows belonging to this connective.

TruthTableRows.Description: One row per input combination per connective: the Strong Kleene semantics, stored as data.

schema.name: TruthTableRowId
schema.Description: Stored identity, e.g. 'negation|null|_|null'.

schema.name: Connective
schema.Description: The connective this row defines.

schema.name: LeftInput
schema.Description: Left/sole input truth value.

schema.name: RightInput
schema.Description: Right input truth value; null for the unary negation.

schema.name: Output
schema.Description: Resulting truth value for this input combination.

schema.name: OutputSymbol
schema.Description: Symbol of the output value.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Readable row, e.g. 'negation: null -> null'.

SetRules.Description: The twelve structural rules of the theory: the eleven classical naive rules plus the missing Rule 12.

schema.name: SetRuleId
schema.Description: Stored identity, e.g. 'rule-12'.

schema.name: RuleNumber
schema.Description: Ordinal 1-12.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Short rule title.

schema.name: Statement
schema.Description: Full statement of the rule.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display alias 'R<n>'.

schema.name: IsClassical
schema.Description: True for the eleven classical rules (R1-R11).

schema.name: IsTheMissingRule
schema.Description: True only for Rule 12, the rule naive set theory never wrote down.

data.Title: Membership
data.Statement: A set is a collection of distinct objects; x in S asserts x is a member of S.

data.Title: Extensionality
data.Statement: A set is fully determined by its members; sets with the same membership profile are identical.

data.Title: Order-independence
data.Statement: The order in which members are listed carries no information.

data.Title: Repetition-independence
data.Statement: Listing a member more than once adds nothing.

data.Title: Two descriptions
data.Statement: A set may be given by enumeration or by a membership condition phi.

data.Title: Comprehension
data.Statement: Any condition you can state determines a set of exactly the objects satisfying it.

data.Title: Unbounded size
data.Statement: A set may have any number of members: none, finitely many, or infinitely many.

data.Title: Empty set
data.Statement: There is a set with no members.

data.Title: Singletons
data.Statement: For any object there is a one-member set, distinct from that member.

data.Title: Nesting
data.Statement: Sets may be members of other sets.

data.Title: Self-membership
data.Statement: A set may be a member of itself; S in S is a well-formed question.

data.Title: NULL membership
data.Statement: If a set's condition loops back on itself with no stable True/False value, x in S = NULL.

Sets.Description: The universe of sets under discussion. Each set is identified by a slug and defined by a membership condition.

schema.name: SetId
schema.Description: Stored identity slug, e.g. 'russell-set'.

schema.name: Label
schema.Description: Human notation for the set, e.g. '{a}', 'R', '∅'.

schema.name: ConditionExpression
schema.Description: The membership condition (the predicate / definition) phi defining the set.

schema.name: IsSelfReferential
schema.Description: Whether the condition refers to the set's own membership.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display alias.

schema.name: IsRussellSet
schema.Description: Flags the Russell set R = { x | x not-in x }.

schema.name: CountOfMemberships
schema.Description: Number of recorded membership facts where this set is the container.

schema.name: CountOfNullMemberships
schema.Description: Membership facts on this set that are ungrounded (NULL).

schema.name: Memberships
schema.Description: Membership facts for which this set is the container.

data.Label: U
data.ConditionExpression: x is a set (U includes itself)

MembershipFacts.Description: The core relation: each fact is a question 'Element in Container' carrying its three-valued answer.

schema.name: MembershipFactId
schema.Description: Stored identity, e.g. 'russell-set-in-russell-set'.

schema.name: Element
schema.Description: The candidate member (itself a set).

schema.name: Container
schema.Description: The set being asked about.

schema.name: MembershipValue
schema.Description: The three-valued answer to Element in Container.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Readable fact, e.g. 'russell-set ∈ russell-set'.

schema.name: ContainerLabel
schema.Description: Notation of the container set.

schema.name: MembershipValueSymbol
schema.Description: Symbol (T/F/N) of the answer.

schema.name: IsBivalent
schema.Description: Whether the answer is one of the classical values (not NULL).

schema.name: IsNull
schema.Description: True when the fact is ungrounded (NULL).

schema.name: IsGrounded
schema.Description: True when the fact settles to True or False.

schema.name: CountOfEvaluationSteps
schema.Description: Number of evaluation trials recorded for this fact.

schema.name: EvaluationSteps
schema.Description: The fixed-point evaluation trials for this fact.

EvaluationSteps.Description: Strong Kleene fixed-point evaluation of the Russell fact: each trial value fed through phi = not(R in R). Only NULL is stable, dissolving the Russell paradox into a single ungrounded fixed point.

schema.name: EvaluationStepId
schema.Description: Stored identity of the trial.

schema.name: MembershipFact
schema.Description: The fact being evaluated.

schema.name: StepOrder
schema.Description: Trial order (1,2,3).

schema.name: TrialValue
schema.Description: The truth value assumed for R in R before applying phi.

schema.name: ResultingValue
schema.Description: The value phi = not(R in R) yields under that assumption.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Readable trial.

schema.name: IsStable
schema.Description: True when the trial reproduces itself (a fixed point).

schema.name: Outcome
schema.Description: 'stable fixed point' or 'contradiction'.

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Schema-first three-valued set theory; membership is a TruthValues-valued relation, Strong Kleene tables stored as data, Rule 12 makes ungrounded facts NULL. Per CMCC, this is a declarative system in which every declarable property has an evaluation outcome (possibly NULL).

_conversion_metadata.source: Shadle bootstrap from article 'A Missing Rule in Set Theory'
